# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:  # Define stages (build and deployment) to run jobs on the pipeline from
    - build
    - deploy

variables:   # Variable definitions for use in other steps of a job/stage or across all projects. These are not encrypted, so they can be seen by anyone who has access to your project's repository (and thus logs).  You should only store sensitive data here that you do NOT want exposed publicly
    GITLAB_URL: 'https://gitlab.com'   # URL of the gitLab instance where we will create our projects and run jobs on it, this is not encrypted or stored in plaintext so anyone who has access to your project can see its content (and thus logs) 
    
    PROJECTS_PATH: '/path/to/projects'   # Path for storing the gitLab CI configuration files. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    ARTIFACTS_PATH: '/path/to/artifacts'   # Path for storing the build artifacts. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    ARTIFACTS_STORAGE: '30d'   # Artifacts are stored for this long. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    SECRETS_PATH: '/path/to/secrets'   # Path to store secrets for CI pipeline. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    TOKENS_PATH: '/path/to/tokens'   # Path to store tokens for CI pipeline. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    SALT_PATH: '/path/to/salts'   # Path to store salts for CI pipeline. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_TOKEN: 'your-gitlab-token'   # Token for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_USER: 'your-gitlab-user'   # User for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_GROUP: 'your-gitlab-group'   # Group for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_PROJECT: 'your-gitlab-project'   # Project for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_ENV: 'your-gitlab-env'   # Environment for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_REGISTRY: 'your-gitlab-registry'   # Registry for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_REGISTRY_IMAGE: 'your-gitlab-registry-image'   # Registry image for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_REGISTRY_USER: 'your-gitlab-registry-user'   # Registry user for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_REGISTRY_PASSWORD: 'your-gitlab-registry-password'   # Registry password for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs) 
    
    GITLAB_CI: 'your-gitlab-ci'   # GitLab ci for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs)

    GITLAB_CI: 'your-gitlab-ci'   # GitLab ci for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content (and logs)

    GITLAB_CI: 'your-gitlab-ci'   # GitLab ci for gitLab CI/CD. This should be a secure location and only accessible by you, not others or from outside sources so it is encrypted in plaintext storage but can still see its content
